quick_facts.label: Role scope
quick_facts.value: A Web Developer designs and writes code for web-based applications, websites and portals, maintains them, handles their technical aspects, and helps establish coding standards.

quick_facts.label: Sector
quick_facts.value: India’s National Career Service classifies Web Developer within the IT-ITeS sector and lists related titles including Web Content Management Developer, Web Application Developer and Web Services Developer.

quick_facts.label: Academic routes
quick_facts.value: BCA is a recognised bachelor’s degree entered after 10+2, while MCA is a recognised master’s degree entered after a bachelor’s degree. Programme curricula, admissions and durations can differ by institution and applicable regulations.

quick_facts.label: NIMCET route
quick_facts.value: For the 2026–27 cycle, NIMCET is the national-level entrance test used for MCA admission at the participating 11 NITs and 2 IIITs; applicants should rely on the applicable year’s official brochure and eligibility rules.

quick_facts.label: Core technical capabilities
quick_facts.value: The National Career Service profile identifies web technologies, databases, responsive design, version control, database design, client-server/internet architecture, object-oriented programming and browser compatibility as relevant competencies.

quick_facts.label: Indicative pay context
quick_facts.value: The NCS Web Developer profile, last updated on 9 August 2016, reported indicative monthly earnings of ₹8,000–₹12,500 for new entrants and ₹20,000–₹45,000 for experienced candidates. These are historical reference figures, not a current salary benchmark.

sections.heading: What a Web Developer does
paragraphs: Web development covers building and maintaining web-facing software. In the National Career Service occupational profile, the role includes designing and coding websites, portals and web applications; maintaining those systems; handling technical aspects; and designing, communicating and maintaining coding standards.

In practice, the role can be front-end focused, back-end focused or full-stack. A front-end emphasis concerns the browser-facing interface and responsive behaviour; a back-end emphasis concerns server-side services, APIs, data storage and application logic; full-stack work combines both. The precise split depends on the employer, product and team structure.
bullets: Translate requirements, designs or wireframes into working web pages or application features.
Write, test, debug and maintain code; address browser/device compatibility and responsiveness.
Integrate interfaces with back-end services, APIs and databases where the role requires it.
Use version control and follow team coding standards, review practices and delivery processes.
Improve reliability, maintainability and performance over the life of a website or web application.

sections.heading: Entry routes in the Indian education system
paragraphs: A common academic route is BCA followed by entry-level web-development work, portfolio building and internships. UGC’s degree specification lists BCA as a bachelor’s degree with 10+2 as the minimum entry qualification, so it is an identifiable undergraduate parent route for this node.

MCA can deepen computing preparation before or after initial work experience. It is a postgraduate route after a bachelor’s degree; however, applicants must check the eligibility, curriculum, duration and admission rules of each institution rather than assuming one uniform national pattern.

NIMCET is an admission examination, not a web-development certification or a job. In the 2026 cycle, it is used for MCA admissions at the participating NITs and IIITs. Its schedules, participating institutes, eligibility and counselling rules are cycle-specific, so candidates should use the official year-specific information brochure.

A skills-first route is also possible: learn the relevant technologies, build deployable projects, obtain practical experience through internships or client work, and demonstrate work through a portfolio and code samples. The NCS profile notes that web-designing/development internship or work experience is generally relevant for the occupation.
bullets: After 10+2: pursue a BCA or another relevant computing programme, while building practical web projects.
After a bachelor’s degree: pursue MCA where it fits career goals; NIMCET is one route to MCA at its participating institutes.
Alongside either route: develop a portfolio, use version control, practise debugging and responsive development, and seek internship or junior developer opportunities.
GATE may be relevant for some postgraduate admissions or recruitment pathways, but qualifying it does not itself guarantee admission, scholarship or employment.

sections.heading: Skills to develop
paragraphs: A Web Developer needs both implementation skills and delivery discipline. The NCS profile specifically names HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript, responsive web design, PHP and .NET examples, along with DBMS/MySQL, database architecture, version control, client-server and internet architecture, XML schemas, object-oriented programming and browser-specific compatibility work. Tool choices change over time, but these underlying capability areas remain useful for evaluating a learning plan.

The role also requires communication and collaboration: understanding requirements, estimating work, incorporating client or stakeholder feedback, and working independently or in teams are all reflected in the NCS profile.
bullets: Front end: semantic HTML, CSS, JavaScript, responsive layouts and cross-browser testing.
Back end and data: server-side programming concepts, APIs, authentication concepts, database modelling and SQL/DBMS fundamentals.
Engineering workflow: version control, readable code, debugging, testing habits, documentation and deployment awareness.
Product delivery: requirement interpretation, estimation, feedback incorporation and clear communication with designers, project managers and clients.
Portfolio evidence: completed projects that show responsive interfaces, data/API integration, maintainable source code and problem-solving decisions.

sections.heading: Work setting and collaboration
paragraphs: The NCS occupational profile describes Web Developer as primarily a desk-based role and notes that work-from-home may be available. It also indicates that work hours, shift arrangements and overtime vary by organisation. These are role-level indicators rather than guarantees for any employer or employment contract.

Web developers commonly collaborate with design, content and project functions. An NCS-listed website-developer vacancy describes working with UI/UX designers, content creators and project managers, as well as integrating with back-end services, APIs and databases. That vacancy is an example of employer expectations, not a universal requirement.
bullets: Typical environments include IT services firms, software product companies, digital agencies, internal enterprise technology teams and independent client work.
Work may be office-based, hybrid or remote depending on the employer and assignment.
Expect coordination with UI/UX, QA, content, product and delivery stakeholders; team size and on-call or release expectations vary.

sections.heading: Progression and adjacent paths
paragraphs: The NCS profile presents an indicative progression from Engineer Trainee to Web Developer, Senior Web Developer, Web Solution Developer, Delivery Lead, Offering/Domain Lead and Head of Company/CEO. It also identifies project management as a possible occupational move. Actual titles and promotion criteria vary substantially by employer.

The node’s listed children should be read as possible next steps rather than a compulsory ladder. Examples include progressing toward senior software engineering, freelancing, product or design-adjacent work, an MCA, or entrepreneurship. CAT and GATE are examinations that may support particular education or career choices; they are not job titles or automatic promotions.
bullets: Deepen technical scope: senior developer or senior software-engineering roles.
Broaden delivery scope: technical lead, solution development, delivery leadership or project/product-facing work.
Change working model: freelance web development or founding a technology venture.
Continue education: MCA through institution-specific admissions; NIMCET is a route for participating NIT/IIIT MCA programmes.
Use entrance examinations purposefully: GATE can support selected postgraduate or PSU-related opportunities, but does not guarantee admission or a job.

sections.heading: Compensation context
paragraphs: Salary depends on location, employer type, skill depth, prior experience, work arrangement, technology stack and the ability to demonstrate production-quality work. No single figure should be treated as a current market rate across India.

For dated historical context only, the National Career Service Web Developer profile—last updated on 9 August 2016—listed indicative earnings of ₹8,000–₹12,500 per month for new entrants and ₹20,000–₹45,000 per month for candidates with experience, while explicitly stating that the figures were indicative and subject to change. These figures should not be used to set a 2026 salary expectation; candidates should compare current, location-specific offers and the complete compensation terms.
bullets: Assess an offer using fixed pay, variable pay, benefits, work location, probation terms, notice period, learning support and expected hours—not base pay alone.
For freelance work, assess compensation against scope, revision limits, maintenance obligations, payment milestones and tax/compliance responsibilities.
Treat salary data from old occupational profiles as historical context, not a live benchmark.
